heading: Protection of nature of Agency’s functions
label: 3507 Protection of nature of Agency’s functions
text: In the interests of the security of the foreign intelligence activities of the United States and in order further to implement that the Director of National Intelligence shall be responsible for protecting intelligence sources and methods from unauthorized disclosure, the Agency shall be exempted from the provisions of sections 1 and 2 of the Act of (, 957; ), and the provisions of any other law which require the publication or disclosure of the organization or functions of the Agency, or of the names, official titles, salaries, or numbers of personnel employed by the Agency: , That in furtherance of this section, the Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall make no reports to the Congress in connection with the Agency under , as amended ().section 3024(h) of this title49 Stat. 9565 U.S.C. 654section 607 of the Act of June 30, 19455 U.S.C. 947(b)August 28, 1935Provided
notes: References in TextAct of , referred to in text, which provided for the yearly publication of the Official Register of the United States, was repealed by , , .August 28, 1935July 12, 1960Pub.
